content: Vercel remains the standard/default host for Next.js in 2026 (built by the same company, zero-config deploys, edge network, image optimization, preview deployments). Hobby (free) tier: 100GB bandwidth, 1M function invocations, 4 CPU-hrs compute, 5,000 free image transformations, but per official docs (vercel.com/docs/plans/hobby, fair-use guidelines) it is restricted to non-commercial personal use only -- a revenue-generating SaaS (even pre-monetization, freemium product for resellers) falls outside Hobby's terms and should budget for Pro (/month per seat) for the production deployment, while Hobby remains fine for a personal preview/staging project during development.
